topic: A technician performing a 'Three-Point Bend' test on a carbon fiber sample.
raw_generation: {"script":"Scene 1 — Opening (0-2s): Side view: technician approaches the three-point bending rig, aligns the carbon fiber sample on the supports.\nScene 2 — Action (2-5s): Front macro: technician clamps the sample, applies middle-load with slow, increasing deflection; camera holds on the carbon fiber sample under load.\nScene 3 — Close (5-8s): Tilted close-up: deflection grows; the technician notes the peak readout on a small display; On-screen text: Three-Point Bend Test Complete.","narration":"The carbon fiber sample sits in a fixed rig as the technician applies load along the center. Three-point bending reveals stiffness, bending tolerance, and potential microcrack initiation, so precise alignment is essential. The sequence from setup to readout translates lab data into material properties viewers can grasp. Subtle camera moves—side view, macro close-up, tilt—highlight how small deflections relate to structural behavior."}

parsed_generation.script:
Scene 1 — Opening (0-2s): Side view: technician approaches the three-point bending rig, aligns the carbon fiber sample on the supports.
Scene 2 — Action (2-5s): Front macro: technician clamps the sample, applies middle-load with slow, increasing deflection; camera holds on the carbon fiber sample under load.
Scene 3 — Close (5-8s): Tilted close-up: deflection grows; the technician notes the peak readout on a small display; On-screen text: Three-Point Bend Test Complete.
parsed_generation.narration: The carbon fiber sample sits in a fixed rig as the technician applies load along the center. Three-point bending reveals stiffness, bending tolerance, and potential microcrack initiation, so precise alignment is essential. The sequence from setup to readout translates lab data into material properties viewers can grasp. Subtle camera moves—side view, macro close-up, tilt—highlight how small deflections relate to structural behavior.

feedback: The topic is well-represented and the narration adds useful context. Consider aligning time stamps and shot labels more strictly with a minimal, task-first flow.
strengths: Explicit three-point bend elements (alignment, clamping, load, readout)
fixes: Streamline scene descriptors to action-first language (setup, clamp, apply load, readout) and reduce extraneous camera notes
Add a definitive end-state moment (e.g., 'Test Complete: pass/fail' or readout summary) and tighten time stamps
